proved: Scenario-level notes and scenario/turn-scoped flags retain immutable create/edit/resolve/reopen events with participant ID, display-name snapshot, timestamp, and exact parent.
Product edit/resolve/reopen operations require the exact current event, reject stale writes, and treat exact event replay idempotently.
Disconnected peers' first annotations survive in separate versioned discussion buckets across forty duplicate/reordered deliveries.
Concurrent edit and resolve children remain in history and converge one deterministic current lifecycle across another forty duplicate/reordered deliveries.
Colliding public annotation identity, malformed event/bucket graphs, and missing scenario or turn targets fail closed or surface as explicit integrity issues.
MCP research-state inspection returns annotation identity/kind/status/count/timestamps and integrity only; synthetic body and display-name canaries are omitted.
P-20 is implemented_unverified; no annotations UI, authentication, moderation, notification, or remote-provider claim is made.
notProved: Authenticated participant identity, authorization, or a trusted distributed clock.
Visible flag/note controls, keyboard/pointer/accessibility behavior, moderation, notifications, or branch reconciliation UI.
Two-machine Drive/WebSocket collaboration or large-annotation-set performance.
Automatic policy mutation, model evaluation, labels, examples, or scenario-pack export.
